title: 10 best herbal teas for sore throat
intro_desc: Herbal teas for sore throat are infusions made from various plants and spices, known for their soothing, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. These teas help by providing relief from pain, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections that cause sore throats. Examples of herbal teas that aid in sore throat relief include chamomile, licorice root, slippery elm, marshmallow root, and ginger. By drinking these teas, individuals can alleviate the discomfort and irritation caused by a sore throat, allowing them to speak, swallow, and eat more comfortably. This, in turn, improves their overall well-being and quality of life, particularly during cold and flu season.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tea helps with sore throat because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the throat. The herb contains mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation. Additionally, mullein tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections causing sore throats. Its demulcent and expectorant effects can also help loosen and expel mucus, providing further relief from symptoms associated with sore throats. Drinking mullein tea can provide a natural and soothing remedy for a sore throat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Great mullein tea can help reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief from pain and irritation, due to its demulcent and anti-inflammatory constituents like verbascoside and mucilage.
Expectorant: The tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and making it easier to breathe, thanks to its saponin content which aids in mucus elimination.
Analgesic: Great mullein tea can help soothe a sore throat by numbing the area, reducing discomfort and pain, due to the presence of analgesic compounds like verbascoside.

Antimicrobial: The tea has the ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat throat infections, as a result of its antimicrobial properties from compounds like rotenone and tannins.

Demulcent: Great mullein tea can form a protective film over the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and providing a soothing effect, due to its high mucilage content which creates a barrier between the mucous membranes and external irritants.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 dried mullein leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose leaf teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the mullein leaves, allowing them to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste for added soothing and flavor.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat and soothe your sore throat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of great mullein are commonly used to make medicinal teas for sore throat due to their demulcent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritation and reduce swelling.
Flowers: Great mullein flowers are used in teas for sore throat because of their soothing and expectorant properties, which can help clear mucus and ease coughing.
Buds: The buds of great mullein contain high levels of mucilage, making them an effective ingredient in teas for sore throat, as they can help soothe and protect the throat lining.
Seeds: Great mullein seeds are used in teas for sore throat due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help clear mucus and reduce swelling.
Roots: The roots of great mullein are used in teas for sore throat because of their demulcent and astringent properties, which can help soothe irritation and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with sore throat because it contains a mucilaginous substance called mucilage, which becomes slippery and thick when mixed with water. This substance coats the throat, forming a protective barrier that soothes irritation, reduces inflammation, and alleviates pain. Moreover, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties that help fight off infections and promote healing. Its soothing and calming effects make it an ideal drink for people suffering from a sore throat, whether it is caused by a cold, flu, or other respiratory conditions. Drinking marshmallow tea can help reduce the discomfort and speed up the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tea is rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from sore throat discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in marshmallow tea help reduce inflammation, alleviating pain and swelling in the throat.
Antitussive: Marshmallow tea can help suppress coughs by soothing the respiratory tract, offering relief for sore throat caused by persistent coughing.
Expectorant: The mucilage in marshmallow tea helps thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion in the throat.
Hydration: Drinking marshmallow tea keeps the throat moist, preventing dryness and irritation, which can exacerbate sore throat symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of dried slippery elm bark.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves, and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then stir in 1-2 teaspoons of honey for added sore throat relief.
Serve the tea hot, and drink it 2-3 times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds, marshmallow leaves help soothe sore throats.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers contain mucilage, which forms a protective layer on mucous membranes, providing relief for sore throats.
Roots: High in mucilage, marshmallow roots create a soothing coating on the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow tea helps with sore throat because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can provide relief to a sore and irritated throat. The mucilage content in high mallow tea creates a protective layer over the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and providing a coating that helps to alleviate pain. Additionally, high mallow tea has been traditionally used to treat respiratory conditions, making it a popular choice for those seeking natural relief from a sore throat. Its mild flavor and soothing effects make it an ideal drink for those looking for a comforting and natural remedy for a sore throat.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: High mallow tea is effective at soothing and protecting irritated mucous membranes in the throat by creating a protective film, thanks to its high mucilage content.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea helps reduce inflammation in the throat, providing pain relief, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antitussive: High mallow tea can help suppress coughing fits, alleviating discomfort for those with sore throats, as it contains iridoid glycosides with known antitussive properties.
Expectorant: The tea aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear obstructions and reduce irritation, due to its saponin content.
Analgesic: High mallow tea can help alleviate pain associated with a sore throat, thanks to the presence of tannins and flavonoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried high mallow leaves or flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for a sore throat.
remedy_parts: Flowers: High mallow flowers are rich in mucilage, which can help soothe a sore throat by forming a protective coating.
Leaves: High mallow leaves are also a good source of mucilage and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation in the throat.
Seeds: High mallow seeds contain high levels of mucilage and can help relieve dry coughs and soothe a sore throat.
Roots: High mallow roots have demulcent properties and can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat.
Bark: The bark of high mallow contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with sore throat because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. This substance can help reduce swelling in the throat, alleviating pain and discomfort. Additionally, licorice tea is known to have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus, providing relief from congestion and coughing. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to soothe a sore throat by increasing blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and providing instant relief. Drinking licorice tea can be a natural and effective way to find comfort during a sore throat episode.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice tea is slippery and coats the throat, protecting the mucous membranes and soothing irritation. This property comes from the high mucilage content in licorice root.
Expectorant: Licorice tea helps to loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and expel. This is due to the glycyrrhizin compound found in licorice root.
Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea reduces inflammation in the throat, alleviating pain and discomfort. The flavonoids and triterpenoids in licorice root contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat throat infections. The glycyrrhizin and flavonoids in licorice root have antimicrobial properties.

Antioxidant: Licorice tea neutralizes free radicals and supports the immune system, promoting overall health and well-being. The flavonoids and chalcones in licorice root provide antioxidant benefits.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for every 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root.
Reduce heat, add licorice root to water, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root pieces.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for sore throat relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help relieve sore throat irritation.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are often used in teas for their demulcent qualities, providing a coating effect on the throat to alleviate pain and discomfort.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant properties, helping to expel mucus and soothe sore throats.
Leaves: Licorice leaves are rich in flavonoids and contain antioxidants, making them useful for treating sore throats and reducing inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip tea helps with sore throat because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes the throat, providing instant relief. It also has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help reduce inflammation and expel mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing irritation. Additionally, cowslip tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections causing the sore throat. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate pain and discomfort, making it an ideal natural remedy for a sore throat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cowslip tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the throat, providing relief from pain and discomfort. This is due to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Expectorant: Cowslip tea acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, easing congestion and soothing a sore throat. This is attributed to the mucilage content in cowslip.

Analgesic: Cowslip tea has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a sore throat. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, flavonoids, and tannins.
Antispasmodic: Cowslip tea has antispasmodic properties that help relax muscles in the throat, reducing coughing and spasms. This is due to the presence of alkaloids and saponins.
Antimicrobial: Cowslip tea has antimicrobial properties that help fight infections causing a sore throat. This is attributed to the presence of tannins, saponins, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried cowslip flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey to taste, for added soothing properties.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat for relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Cowslip flowers are used in medicinal teas for sore throat due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: Cowslip leaves are often used in teas for sore throat as they contain mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the throat.
Roots: The roots of cowslip plants are used in medicinal teas for sore throat because they contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with sore throat because it contains bioflavonoids and other proteins that have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat. The antioxidant properties of elderberry may also help to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that can cause a sore throat. Additionally, elderberry tea has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help to eliminate the viruses and bacteria that cause throat irritation. The soothing and calming effects of the warm tea can also provide instant relief to a sore and scratchy throat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tea can help reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief from pain and irritation, due to its active constituents such as flavonoids and anthocyanins.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of elderberry tea helps protect the throat's mucous membranes from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection.

Immune-boosting: Elderberry tea has been shown to enhance the immune system's response, allowing the body to fight off viral and bacterial infections that can cause a sore throat, thanks to its active compounds like anthocyanins and vitamins A and C.

Antiviral: Elderberry tea contains compounds that can directly inhibit the growth and replication of viruses, including those that cause the common cold and flu, which can lead to a sore throat, due to its active constituents like hemagglutinin inhibitors and neuraminidase inhibitors.

Expectorant: Elderberry tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe, which can alleviate the discomfort of a sore throat, due to its active constituents like tannins and mucilage.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries, bringing the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat, then stir in honey (to taste) and the juice of half a lemon.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a mug, pressing on the elderberries to extract all liquid.
Serve the tea warm, sipping slowly as needed for sore throat relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Elderberry flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing a sore throat.
Berries: Elderberries are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can help boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of a sore throat.
Bark: The bark of elderberry plants contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe a sore throat.
Leaves: Elderberry leaves have been used traditionally for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating a sore throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with sore throat because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the throat. The tea is rich in vitamin C and antioxidants that can boost the immune system and help the body fight off infections. Additionally, the warm temperature of the tea can provide soothing relief to a sore throat, while the herbal properties can help to reduce inflammation and irritation. Drinking large-leaved lime tea can also help to keep the throat hydrated, which is important for healing and preventing further irritation. Overall, large-leaved lime tea is a natural and effective remedy for soothing a sore throat and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids that help reduce inflammation, which is important for sore throat as it can help alleviate pain and swelling.
Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties due to its vitamin C and flavonoid content, helping to fight off infection-causing bacteria and viruses that can lead to a sore throat.
Analgesic: Large-leaved lime tea contains compounds with pain-relieving properties, which can help soothe the discomfort caused by a sore throat.
Antioxidant: The tea is rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and flavonoids, that help protect cells from damage, boost the immune system, and promote healing in the throat.
Demulcent: Large-leaved lime tea has a soothing effect on mucous membranes, creating a protective film that helps reduce irritation and inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 3 tablespoons of dried large-leaved lime flowers, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
In a separate pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil and add 2 teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves, steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Crush 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger and add it to the large-leaved lime flower tea, letting it steep for an additional 3-5 minutes.
Strain both teas, then combine them in a large pot and mix in 1 tablespoon of honey and 2 tablespoons of lemon juice.
Gently heat the mixture on low heat for 2-3 minutes, ensuring it doesn't boil, then pour into a mug and serve.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the large-leaved lime plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for sore throat due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation.
Bark: The bark of the large-leaved lime plant contains tannins and essential oils, which can help relieve irritation and reduce swelling in the throat.
Buds: The buds of the plant are a concentrated source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for treating sore throats and other respiratory conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with sore throat because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can alleviate inflammation and fight off infection. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the linden tree, which contain flavonoids and mucilage that can soothe a sore throat and reduce irritation. Additionally, the tea can help to stimulate perspiration, which can help to eliminate toxins from the body and reduce fever. Drinking littleleaf linden tea can provide relief from a sore throat and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing relief from soreness. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antimicrobial: The tea exhibits antimicrobial properties that can help combat throat infections caused by bacteria or viruses, contributing to sore throat alleviation. This is attributed to the presence of tannins, which have antimicrobial effects.

Expectorant: Littleleaf linden tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and discomfort in the throat. This is due to the presence of mucilage, which has expectorant properties.

Analgesic: The tea has analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and provide soothing relief for a sore throat. This is attributed to the presence of various compounds, such as flavonoids and volatile oils, which have analgesic effects.

Antioxidant: Littleleaf linden tea contains antioxidants that can help protect throat tissues from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and other antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 2 cups of water and let it cool down for a few minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water (but not boiling) over the linden flowers and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers and discard them.
Drink the herbal tea while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day, to soothe a sore throat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, the leaves are often used in teas to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, flowers are used in teas to help alleviate sore throat symptoms.
Buds: High in tannins, buds are used in medicinal teas to help reduce irritation and inflammation in the throat.
Bark: Known for its astringent properties, bark is used in teas to help relieve sore throat and ease coughing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with sore throat because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the throat. The herb contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help boost the immune system and protect against infection. Additionally, it has been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and coughing. Drinking this tea can help reduce the discomfort and duration of a sore throat, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains echinacea, which helps reduce inflammation in the throat by limiting the production of certain chemicals in the body that cause inflammation.

Immune system stimulation: This tea stimulates the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that can cause a sore throat, due to its active constituents, such as alkamides and polysaccharides.
Pain relief: Eastern purple coneflower tea has analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort in the throat, thanks to its rosmarinic acid content.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate a sore throat; these antioxidants include cichoric acid and chicoric acid.

Antimicrobial: Eastern purple coneflower tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help the body fight off the bacteria and viruses that can cause a sore throat, due to its caffeic acid and essential oils content.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers and add to a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool slightly for 2-3 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the coneflower flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Sip the warm tea slowly, allowing it to soothe a sore throat.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help soothe sore throats and support immune function.
Leaves: Packed with soothing mucilage and antibacterial properties, the leaves can provide relief for sore throats and fight off infections.
Roots: Containing anti-catarrhal and anti-inflammatory agents, the roots help alleviate sore throat symptoms and reduce inflammation.
Buds: High in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, the buds can help reduce sore throat pain and fight off pathogens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with sore throat because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off infection. Thyme contains compounds such as thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to have antimicrobial effects against a variety of bacteria and viruses. Additionally, thyme tea can help soothe a sore throat by providing a warm and comforting sensation. Drinking thyme tea can also help increase saliva production, which can help keep the throat moist and relieve dryness. Overall, thyme tea can provide natural relief for a sore throat and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the presence of harmful bacteria and viruses in the throat, which can cause sore throats. This is due to the presence of thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to have antimicrobial effects.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief from sore throat symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antispasmodic: Thyme tea has antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the throat, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing and providing relief from sore throat symptoms. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, which have antispasmodic effects.

Analgesic: Thyme tea has analgesic properties that help reduce pain and discomfort in the throat, providing relief from sore throat symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils, which have analgesic effects.

Expectorant: Thyme tea has expectorant properties that help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from sore throat symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils, which have expectorant effects.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh thyme to the hot water.
Cover and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink while warm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for sore throat due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and volatile oils, making them effective in soothing sore throats and reducing inflammation.
Stems: Although less potent than leaves and flowers, thyme stems can still provide some relief for sore throats, as they contain similar beneficial compounds.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sore throat with herbal teas is by choosing teas with antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. Look for ingredients such as licorice root, ginger, chamomile, slippery elm, and echinacea. Licorice root soothes inflammation, ginger reduces pain and fights infection, chamomile has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, slippery elm coats and soothes the throat, and echinacea boosts the immune system. Steep the tea for 10-15 minutes and sip it slowly for best results. Adding raw honey can further enhance the soothing and antibacterial properties of the tea.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sore throat that are treated with herbal teas include laryngitis, pharyngitis, and tonsillitis. Herbal teas like chamomile, licorice root, and slippery elm can help soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the throat. They can also help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and difficulty swallowing. Additionally, these teas can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that cause these ailments. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
